title: IPv6 single-stack with just link scope addressing
body:
### Feature request description

It would be useful, for security reasons to be able to setup some networks where only IPv6 is enabled (so single-stack, I know this is already possible) but where only IPv6 link scope addresses exist. In other words, do not assign any IPv6 address to the containers / pods.

Some containers / pods  would need to act as some sort of proxy and hence would be connected to a 2nd network but that beyond the scope of this enhancement request.

### Suggest potential solution

Simply add an option on "podman network create" to support IPv6 without defining a subnet. Currently, such command results in a failure with the ipv6 flag is present (which, BTW, is weird since it should be able to create an ULA subnet, but, this is also outside the scope of this request).

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

This would permit to fully isolate the containers / pods from any direct external access WITHOUT having to setup firewall rules, which may fail or get misconfigured at a later time. 

In other words, work in additive / addition mode rather than negative / removal mode. The former is much safer and greatly simplify devSecOps.

comments.body:
> Simply add an option on "podman network create" to support IPv6 without defining a subnet. Currently, such command results in a failure with the ipv6 flag is present (which, BTW, is weird since it should be able to create an ULA subnet, but, this is also outside the scope of this request).

What is the error? Please provide the full commands.

If you do not want to assign ips you can use `--ipam-driver none` which I believe is what you asking for if I understand it correctly.

I am on Ubuntu / Redhat / Fedora and the podman version does not support --ipam-driver. The error I am getting is:
podman network create --ipv6
Error: ipv6 option requires an IPv6 --subnet to be provided

But I managed to get access to a newer podman and, indeed, --ipamdriver node (coupled with the --ipv6 flag) gives me exactly what I wanted: an IPv6 single-stack iwht link scope addresses.

I will close the ticket.
